bull.b: Digital Realty’s steady top-line expansion and recovery of net margins to ~21% indicate sustainable operating leverage in its core leasing business. This improves FFO generation potential, supports dividend coverage and funds continued development, making earnings less dependent on short-term cyclical swings.

A record $1.8B backlog plus landmark 200 MW hyperscale signing validate demand for large-scale capacity and give multiyear revenue visibility. High-prelease rates and contracted commencements reduce rollout risk and underpin accretive returns on development investments over the medium term.

Lower leverage and substantial private capital dry powder enhance financial flexibility to execute large hyperscale projects without over-reliance on public markets. Improved debt metrics and partner capital lower refinancing vulnerability and support disciplined scaling of the development pipeline.

bear.b: Despite ratio improvements, a ~$19.2B absolute debt stock leaves the company exposed to refinancing, interest-rate and covenant risks. Large nominal leverage constrains discretionary spending and increases sensitivity of returns and FFO to rises in borrowing costs or slower leasing activity.

Historical FCF volatility and prior negative free cash flow reflect heavy development spending and timing mismatches. With TTM conversion weaker (~45% of net income), reliance on equity/JV funding remains necessary, reducing internal funding resilience for dividends or opportunistic investments.

Higher per‑MW build costs (from ~$12.5M to ~$14M) compress prospective yields and raise break-even thresholds for new hyperscale projects. Combined with longer commencement lags for very large deals, this increases project execution risk and can delay cash flow realization from development investments.
